evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/DMI requester
evidence_slots.operation: DMI write/read sequence to debug control and command registers causing halt/resume/debug-entry behavior
evidence_slots.object: hart debug control state: dm_csrs haltreq_o/resumereq_o, dm_top debug_req_o via dm_mem, and dmcontrol state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI operations that can halt/resume harts or enter debug mode should be mediated by debug authentication/authorization, lifecycle enable, or a configured jtag_key check before asserting debug requests.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs sets dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1 with comment 'no authentication implemented' and accepts DMI requests whenever dmi_req_valid_i and dmi_req_ready_o are true. DMControl writes assign dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data; haltreq_o[selected_hart] and resumereq_o[selected_hart] are directly driven from dmcontrol_q.haltreq/resumereq. dm_top connects these to dm_mem, which drives debug_req_o. The visible dm_csrs request-generation logic contains no auth/lifecycle predicate other than normal DMI handshaking and dmactive reset semantics.
evidence_slots.path: fpga/openpiton JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag TAP/CDC -> dm_top dmi_req_i -> dm_csrs DMControl/Command decode -> haltreq_o/resumereq_o -> dm_mem -> debug_req_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmcontrol_q resets to zero; when dmactive is low most dmcontrol fields are synchronously cleared, but dmactive itself is a DMI-controlled bit. dmstatus.authenticated is statically high. dmi_jtag has a jtag_key input and pass register copied on reset, but the provided observations do not close whether it gates dmi_req_valid_o before CDC.
evidence_slots.impact: A physical JTAG/DMI actor can request hart halt/resume or debug entry, suspending normal execution and enabling invasive debug control of protected CPU state without an observed authorization decision at the debug CSR layer.

rtl_evidence.detail: dmstatus.version set and dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1; comment says no authentication implemented.
DMI response/request handshaking uses FIFO readiness; resp_queue_push = dmi_req_valid_i & dmi_req_ready_o.
DMControl write casts dmi_req_i.data and assigns dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data.
haltreq_o and resumereq_o selected by hartsel_o and directly driven from dmcontrol_q.haltreq/resumereq.
dm_mem receives haltreq_i/resumereq_i and outputs debug_req_o.
dmi_cdc connects JTAG-side DMI valid/request to core-side dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The provided dmi_jtag snippet is truncated before the state machine lines where pass_chk may be used; therefore the finding is strongest for the dm_csrs/dm_top layer and remains uncertain for any earlier jtag_key comparison in dmi_jtag.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The debug module CSR layer advertises authentication as always successful and DMI writes can drive hart halt/resume control state, so an external JTAG/DMI requester can reach hart debug control without an observed lifecycle/authentication guard in the inspected request-generation path.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate finding is based on observed always-authenticated debug CSR semantics and direct DMControl-to-halt/resume outputs.

evidence_slots.subject: external debug requester
evidence_slots.operation: write debug DMControl register to assert/deassert non-debug reset or dmactive
evidence_slots.object: ndmreset_o and dmactive_o outputs and connected reset/control domains
evidence_slots.expected_guard: External debug reset and debug-module activation controls should require debug authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable mediation, and reset effects should be limited to intended domains.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: DMControl writes assign dmcontrol_d from dmi_req_i.data. dmactive_o is assigned to dmcontrol_q.dmactive, and ndmreset_o is assigned to dmcontrol_q.ndmreset. No auth/lifecycle condition appears in the observed write decode or output assignment. Reset initializes dmcontrol_q to zero; if dmactive is low most fields are synchronously cleared except dmactive handling, making dmactive itself the DMI-controlled activation bit.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/DMI -> dmi_jtag/dmi_cdc -> dm_top -> dm_csrs DMControl decode -> dmactive_o/ndmreset_o -> top-level reset/control nets
evidence_slots.state_condition: On PoR rst_ni low, dmcontrol_q <= '0. During normal operation, if !dmcontrol_q.dmactive, most debug-control fields are cleared; this does not constitute authorization for writing dmactive. dmi_rst_ni resets the response FIFO but is not shown to mediate DMControl writes.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated debug traffic can reset non-debug execution state or toggle debug-module availability, causing denial of service and potentially disrupting secure boot/runtime state.

rtl_evidence.detail: DMControl write path assigns dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data.
dmactive_o = dmcontrol_q.dmactive and ndmreset_o = dmcontrol_q.ndmreset.
PoR clears dmcontrol_q; synchronous debug reset behavior depends on dmcontrol_q.dmactive.
dm_top exposes ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, and debug_req_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Top-level reset-domain fanout of ndmreset_o is not fully shown in the observations, so exact reset impact/domain boundary remains partially unclosed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI writes to DMControl can directly set dmactive and ndmreset outputs without an observed debug authorization/lifecycle guard, allowing an external debug requester to activate the debug module or assert non-debug reset.
inspection_records.notes: The vulnerable operation is visible at the debug CSR output boundary even if downstream reset fanout varies by integration.

evidence_slots.subject: CPU CSR instruction requester
evidence_slots.object: privilege, translation, trap-vector, debug, cache and performance CSRs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes to machine/supervisor/debug/security-sensitive CSRs should be permitted only from the appropriate privilege mode and should enforce read-only fields and delegation restrictions; user-mode access should trap or be blocked.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: csr_regfile decodes CSR addresses and sets read_access_exception/update_access_exception for unsupported or illegal CSR operations. It performs an explicit privilege check on any CSR read/write using csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl versus priv_lvl_o and raises ILLEGAL_INSTR on mismatch. Debug-mode-only CSRs in address range 0x7b* are additionally blocked unless debug_mode_q is true. Read-only/unsupported writes fall to update_access_exception in the observed write decode.
evidence_slots.path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> csr_read_process/csr_update_process -> privilege and exception checks -> CSR state/output updates
evidence_slots.state_condition: Privilege check is performed whenever csr_we or csr_read is active. Debug CSR access requires debug_mode_q. FPU reads/writes additionally require mstatus.fs not Off. Reset values and commit gating beyond the provided snippet are not fully detailed, but guard logic is present.
evidence_slots.impact: The observed guard causes illegal-instruction exceptions for insufficient privilege or debug-mode-only CSR access, preventing ordinary user CSR operations from being treated as authorized accesses.

rtl_evidence.detail: Read process defaults read_access_exception low and sets it for illegal FCSR read when FS is Off.
Write/update process checks csr_we before case decode.
Default write case sets update_access_exception = 1'b1.
Privilege check raises ILLEGAL_INSTR on privilege mismatch, blocks debug CSRs when not in debug mode, and raises exception for read/update access exceptions.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The provided observations do not fully show the sequential commit/update block, so state-update suppression on exception is inferred from standard CSR exception flow rather than directly observed.
inspection_records.notes: Despite unusual exception for CSR_MEPC in the privilege expression, the requested general CSR privilege/write-decode guard is visibly implemented.

A file comment states direct system bus accesses are not yet possible due to a missing AXI-lite bridge, which limits SBA via NoC but not necessarily NoC access to the debug peripheral window.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* -> serpent_peripherals debug/NoC interface -> debug module AXI/DMI controls -> ariane_debug_buf_noc3_* responses
evidence_slots.state_condition: Ready/valid acceptance policy and reset default of debug routing enables were not shown. testmode_i is a serpent_peripherals input but no secure lifecycle tie-off was observed.
evidence_slots.impact: A NoC-originated actor on the debug segment may be able to access debug registers, observe responses, or drive debug-control operations without equivalent local JTAG/key mediation, potentially bypassing intended debug authorization boundaries.

rtl_evidence.detail: Comment notes direct system bus accesses are not yet possible due to missing AXI-lite bridge to NoC converter.
Comments list Debug address base 40'h90_0000_0000 length 0x1000.
Debug/JTAG NoC2 input and NoC3 output ready/valid/data ports are exposed.
Debug control outputs and JTAG pins are exposed from serpent_peripherals.
serpent_peripherals instantiates i_dmi_jtag.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact internal routing from NoC debug ports to dm_top/dmi_jtag and any NoC filter policy were not shown in the provided observations; the candidate is based on exposed NoC debug window/ports plus absence of observed mediation.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The OpenPiton integration exposes a NoC-facing debug endpoint that routes to the debug module as an AXI slave, and no authentication or requester-policy check is visible in the provided serpent_peripherals evidence before NoC debug traffic reaches debug controls.
inspection_records.notes: The impact is somewhat integration-dependent because OpenPiton NoC filters may exist outside the visible scope.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester
evidence_slots.object: jtag_key signal and backing rom2 secure_reg entry
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes or configuration of jtag_key should be restricted to trusted software/privileged bus masters, protected from unprivileged MMIO writes, and should not be readable or default to a public bypass value.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals connects an AXI memory interface rom2_fuse through axi2mem to rom2 req/we/addr/wdata. rom2 copies constant key values into secure_reg on reset but allows req_i && we_i to write secure_reg at the addressed index. ariane_peripherals assigns jtag_key = key_reg_out[1][31:0]. dmi_jtag copies jtag_key into pass on reset. No lock bit, privilege check, secure-only bus attribute check, or readback restriction is observed; rom2 read returns secure_reg contents.
evidence_slots.path: AXI rom2_fuse -> axi2mem i_axi2rom2 -> rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg/key_reg_out -> ariane_peripherals jtag_key -> dmi_jtag.jtag_key/pass
evidence_slots.state_condition: On reset rom2 secure_reg <= constant mem; thereafter any req_i with we_i writes secure_reg. dmi_jtag pass <= jtag_key only when rst_ni is low in the observed block, so key updates may require reset to take effect in dmi_jtag depending on integration.
evidence_slots.impact: Software or another bus master with access to the rom2_fuse window can overwrite or read key material, weakening JTAG authentication and potentially enabling later debug access or disclosure of authentication secrets.

rtl_evidence.detail: Constant mem stores key values including a JTAG entry.
On reset secure_reg <= mem; otherwise req_i && we_i writes secure_reg[address] <= wdata_i.
rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q] when in range.
axi2mem connects AXI rom2_fuse to rom2_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata.
rom2 secure_reg connects to key_reg_out; jtag_key assigned key_reg_out[1][31:0].
dmi_jtag copies input jtag_key into pass on reset.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Top-level connection from ariane_peripherals.jtag_key to a deployed dmi_jtag instance is not fully shown in the provided observations; the write/read exposure of the key backing store is nevertheless explicit in the integration/test peripheral scope.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The JTAG key is sourced from writable rom2 secure_reg state reachable through an AXI-to-memory interface, and no privilege/lock/readback protection is observed before software-visible writes can alter the key material used by dmi_jtag.
inspection_records.notes: The issue overlaps ROM2 write-protect but is specifically about debug-authentication key integrity/confidentiality.

evidence_slots.subject: bus/MMIO requester reaching rom2_fuse
evidence_slots.operation: write with we_i asserted to rom2
evidence_slots.object: rom2 secure_reg contents and rdata_o/key outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM/boot or security reference contents should be read-only after reset/initialization; any write port should be ignored, blocked, or restricted to an authorized provisioning/test mode.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 initializes secure_reg from constant mem on reset. During normal operation, if req_i is high and we_i is high, secure_reg[index] is overwritten with wdata_i. ariane_peripherals wires AXI rom2_fuse through axi2mem into these signals and exports secure_reg-derived jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg. No guard condition besides req_i/we_i and address index is observed.
evidence_slots.path: AXI rom2_fuse -> tb/ariane_peripherals.i_axi2rom2 -> rom2.req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg -> rdata_o/jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset restores constant mem contents. Writes are allowed after reset whenever req_i && we_i. There is no observed test/provisioning-mode condition, sticky lock, write-once behavior, or tie-off of we_i.
evidence_slots.impact: A bus requester can alter fuse/ROM-like key and access-control constants, enabling modification of JTAG key material and peripheral access-control registers and undermining security assumptions based on immutable ROM/fuse state.

rtl_evidence.detail: Module described as ROM2 containing keys.
Constant mem contains AES/JTAG/access-control values.
secure_reg initialized from mem on reset; normal req_i && we_i overwrites secure_reg[address] with wdata_i.
rdata_o returns secure_reg contents for selected address.
axi2mem and rom2 instance connect AXI rom2_fuse to rom2 request/write/address/data ports.
jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg outputs are derived from key_reg_out/secure_reg.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The global address map and CPU/bus-master privilege controls for rom2_fuse are not shown, so reachability by unprivileged software is not fully proven; the local ROM write behavior is clear.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: rom2 is a ROM-like key/fuse module but supports writes to secure_reg through a bus-connected axi2mem path without any observed provisioning/test authorization, lock bit, or write-disable control.
inspection_records.notes: This is a direct write-protection violation for a ROM/fuse-like module.

evidence_slots.subject: external PCIe/XDMA master
evidence_slots.object: system DRAM and MMIO regions reachable from external PCIe DMA
evidence_slots.expected_guard: External PCIe/DMA masters should be constrained by address-map, IOMMU/PMP-like, or bridge range checks before reading/writing protected DRAM, boot ROM, debug, or MMIO regions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_xilinx instantiates xdma_0 and AXI width/clock converters per static facts and search output. The provided observations do not include the XDMA AXI signal wiring, address decode, interconnect policy, or vendor IP configuration. No visible range check is evidenced, but absence cannot be concluded from the limited snippets.
evidence_slots.path: PCIe pins -> ariane_xilinx.i_xdma -> AXI converters/interconnect -> system memory/peripheral fabric
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset/config defaults of XDMA ranges and preservation of AXI protection attributes are not provided.
evidence_slots.impact: If unconstrained, an external PCIe DMA master could read/write DRAM/MMIO/debug/boot regions outside CPU privilege controls, but the provided evidence does not establish the actual reachable address set or lack of bridge filtering.

rtl_evidence.detail: xdma_0 instance i_xdma exists.
AXI width and clock converter instances associated with PCIe path are present.
Search observed xdma_0 i_xdma instantiation line.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need ariane_xilinx lines around the xdma_0 instance and interconnect/address filtering to determine whether external PCIe DMA reaches protected slaves and whether range checks exist.
inspection_records.notes: Do not classify as violation from instance presence alone.

evidence_slots.subject: CPU CSR instruction requester
evidence_slots.object: perf_addr_o, perf_data_o, perf_we_o, perf_data_i and performance counter CSRs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Performance counters and event selectors that expose privileged activity or affect monitoring should enforce mode-based access controls and counter-enable/inhibit policy.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Performance counter writes occur only inside csr_we decode for machine/performance CSR addresses and assert perf_we_o for specific event CSRs. The common CSR privilege check applies to all csr_we/csr_read operations using csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl and raises ILLEGAL_INSTR on insufficient privilege. perf_we_o defaults to 0, limiting writes to decoded legal operations.
evidence_slots.path: csr_regfile csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> performance CSR decode -> perf_addr_o/perf_data_o/perf_we_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: perf_we_o is default 0 each cycle; privilege check is active for any CSR read/write. Counter-enable policy details such as mcounteren/scounteren are not fully implemented/shown, but machine-level privilege guard is visible for protected performance CSRs.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized lower-privilege writes should raise illegal instruction rather than assert perf_we_o as an authorized performance configuration update.

rtl_evidence.detail: perf_addr_o feeds through CSR address for performance counter access.
perf_we_o and perf_data_o default to 0.
Specific performance CSR cases drive perf_data_o and perf_we_o = 1'b1.
Common CSR privilege and access-exception checks raise ILLEGAL_INSTR.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Counter-enable/inhibit policy for exposing counter reads to lower modes is not fully visible; status is satisfied for the required mode-based CSR privilege guard in the inspected code.
inspection_records.notes: If user/supervisor performance counter read policy is in scope, additional architecture-specific review may be needed.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG reader
evidence_slots.object: DMI response data, DTMCS, IDCODE, bypass state shifted to td_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG shift-out paths should expose only authorized DTM/DMI response data and should not leak internal keys, stale DMI response data, or protected state when DMI access is not selected/authorized.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag_tap output mux selects IR shift, IDCODE, DTMCSR, DMIACCESS dmi_tdo_i, or bypass. tdo_oe_o is asserted only while shifting IR or DR. Reset clears td_o and tdo_oe_o. No observed output mux path exposes jtag_key/pass directly. However DMIACCESS shifts dmi_tdo_i without an observed authorization predicate at the TAP mux; the dmi_jtag response/data shift-register behavior and stale response handling are not fully shown.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag_tap IR/DR scan logic -> tdo_mux -> td_o/tdo_oe_o; DMIACCESS selects dmi_tdo_i from dmi_jtag DMI data path
evidence_slots.state_condition: trst_ni reset clears td_o/tdo_oe_o in observed TAP output flop. testmode_i affects TDO clock selection only in the observed lines. Reset contents of dmi_jtag DMI shift/data registers are partially shown as zero on trst_ni.
evidence_slots.impact: External JTAG can shift out DTMCS and DMI response data when the DMIACCESS instruction is selected; without full response-valid/auth gating evidence, potential stale or unauthorized DMI response exposure cannot be ruled in or out.

rtl_evidence.detail: tdo_mux selects jtag_ir_shift_q[0] during IR shift, otherwise IDCODE, DTMCSR, DMIACCESS dmi_tdo_i, or bypass_q.
td_o <= tdo_mux and tdo_oe_o <= shift_ir | shift_dr_o; reset clears both.
DMI data register shifts dmi_tdi under dmi_access and clears on test_logic_reset.
trst_ni reset clears dr_q, address_q, data_q, and error_q.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full dmi_jtag capture_dr/update_dr logic, dmi_tdo assignment, and dmi_resp_valid/error handling to determine stale data leakage or valid-response gating.
inspection_records.notes: Observed TAP mux is normal for JTAG; leakage finding requires additional evidence not present here.

5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48:
